DetectionRule.Name: Suspicious SMB Lateral Execution
DetectionRule.Description: Detects lateral movement via SMB using WMI, PsExec, or other remote execution tools. Identifies unusual remote service creation and process execution patterns across the network.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1021.002 - SMB/Windows Admin Shares, T1570 - Lateral Tool Transfer

RemediationSteps.Step: Identify source device and isolate if compromised
Investigate targeted devices for signs of compromise
Block suspicious remote execution tools if not required
Implement network segmentation to limit lateral movement

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude approved patching/deployment tools by hash
User-based: Exclude known administrative accounts during maintenance

DetectionRule.Name: WMI Anomalous Execution Pattern
DetectionRule.Description: Detects unusual WMI execution patterns that may indicate lateral movement or living-off-the-land techniques. Monitors wmiprvse.exe child processes, remote WMI execution, and suspicious WQL queries.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1047 - Windows Management Instrumentation, T1046 - Network Service Discovery

RemediationSteps.Step: Investigate parent process that spawned wmiprvse.exe
Review WMI event subscriptions for persistence
Check for unauthorized remote WMI connections
Analyze commands executed through WMI for malicious intent
Review WMI permissions and remove unnecessary access
Monitor for recurring WMI-based lateral movement attempts

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude WMI queries from legitimate admin tools
Exclude WMI usage from approved monitoring tools
Time-based analysis: Focus on off-hours activity

DetectionRule.Name: Remote Service Creation Lateral Movement
DetectionRule.Description: Detects creation of remote services across the network using sc.exe, WMI, or other service creation methods. This is a strong indicator of lateral movement using tools like PsExec or custom malware.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1543.003 - Windows Service, T1021.002 - SMB/Windows Admin Shares

RemediationSteps.Step: Identify and isolate source device creating remote services
Disable any suspicious services that were created
Check targeted devices for additional compromise indicators
Reset credentials for accounts used in lateral movement
Implement service creation monitoring and alerting

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude configuration management tools by hash
Time-based: Alert only during non-maintenance hours

DetectionRule.Name: Pass-the-Hash Lateral Movement Detection
DetectionRule.Description: Detects pass-the-hash attacks where NTLM hashes are used for authentication instead of plaintext passwords. Identifies multiple authentication attempts with the same NTLM hash from different source IPs or machines.
